Get Whatszara
Initializing...

YOUR DESKTOP,
ON WHATSAPP.

A desktop assistant controlled via WhatsApp messages. Talk to an LLM through WhatsApp, and it executes commands on your desktop — shell, apps, media, file access — with a secure permission system.
Built by Preet3627 — MIT Licensed

Get Whatszara Explore Docs View Source

LLM Providers

6

Tool Categories

5

Risk Profiles

3

Languages

2

Core Technologies

Everything
You Need.

WhatsApp-Powered

Control your desktop from any phone via WhatsApp messages. No app to install — it works where you already chat.

Learn More

Multi-LLM Engine

6 providers: Ollama (local), Claude, Groq, Grok, Gemini, Vercel AI SDK. Live model list fetching. Switch providers at runtime.

Learn More

Policy Layer

Propose → Evaluate → Execute. Per-tool permissions, WhatsApp account allowlist, 3 risk profiles with CAPTCHA verification.

Learn More

Structured Actions

No free-form shell. Actions are typed: open_app, set_volume, play_media, send_file. Shell is disabled by default for safety.

Learn More

Undo Journal

Every action is logged with a reverse action. Undo volume changes, pause music, revert file operations with one command.

Learn More

Zero Python

100% Rust + Go. No interpreters, no virtualenvs, no pip. One-click setup.sh installs everything — just Go, Node, and Rust.

Learn More

Open
Source.

Built for the community, by the community. Audit every line of code, contribute new features, or deploy your own private instance.

View Source Fork Project

Download
Whatszara.

Build Environment

v0.1.0

macOS universal + Windows + Linux

Engine
Manual.

Start in seconds

How to Use

From setup to first command — Whatszara gets you running in under a minute. No Python required, zero configuration needed.

Start the WhatsApp bridge with a single command — scan the QR code with your phone to link WhatsApp.
Add your WhatsApp number to the allowlist in the Permissions tab — only approved contacts can control the assistant.
Send a WhatsApp message — the LLM interprets it, the policy engine validates it, and your desktop obeys.
Whatszara Desktop

Your desktop, controlled via WhatsApp. No Python required. Built with Rust + Go.
